figures.caption: Figure 3: Example of our setup (T = 3,m = 4): 1a shows m i.i.d. trajectories generated by xt = gt(x1, . . . , xt−1); 1b shows full data in a matrix, where columns represent trajectories and each row represents data of each task; 1c shows the index sets Rt,Mi and data available at task 3.

Figure 1: Example setup (T = 3,m = 4). Fig. 1a: task dependency (2); Fig. 1b: full data in a matrix, where each column is generated as per (2) and each row represents data of each task; Fig. 1c: index sets Rt and data available at task 3.
